title: Load-time registrar-equivalent clamp for discovered user/minted phase specs (closes the on-disk-spec trust gap the ADR-0073 security review traced)
why: SECURITY-REVIEW-CONFIRMED (ADR-0073 review, Finding 1 downstream trace): phasespec.DiscoverUserSpecsFromRoots (called from cmd_cycle.go) loads any .evolve/phases/*/phase.json straight into the catalog; the only load-time validation is ValidateUserSpecWithCatalog (name floor, optional, kind) — it does NOT re-run the registrar's normalization/clamp: WritesSource=>Sandbox.Enabled forced-on never happens for a spec that bypassed Register, routing_dispatch reads spec.WritesSource straight from the catalog for worktree-write eligibility, and envelope/AllowedCLIs are only consulted at mint time (policy.ValidatePin from phaseregistrar.Register). Consequence: ANY on-disk spec — smuggled pre-abort residue, git merge, operator typo — becomes a schedulable phase with attacker/author-chosen WritesSource and no dispatch clamp. This is the STRUCTURAL hole; the ADR-0073 mint exemption only enforces clamp parity at the guard boundary.
how_to_apply: Single chokepoint: make the discovery/load path run the SAME normalization+clamp Register runs (extract the registrar's normalize+validate block into a shared func consumed by both; phase_settings_from_config_not_code + never_duplicate rules). Forced: Optional=true; WritesSource=>sandbox enabled/not-readonly in the derived profile resolution; reject (loud skip, never a registered dead phase) on ValidateUserSpec violations or unclassifiable tier with an envelope. TDD red-first: a discovered spec with writes_source:true and no sandbox must dispatch SANDBOXED; a spec failing the floor must be skipped loudly, not cataloged. Gate-wiring proof: the clamp must run in the composed cmd_cycle discovery path, not just a unit.
evidence: go/internal/phasespec/discover.go:76-91; go/internal/phasespec/validate.go:75-127; go/internal/phaseregistrar/registrar.go:59-98 (the clamp that never runs at load); go/internal/core/routing_dispatch.go:191-206 (WritesSource consumed from catalog); ADR-0073 security-review Finding 1.
